title: Blue Whale
facts: A blue whale's heart is as big as a small car!
Blue whales are the largest animals that have ever lived on Earth.
A baby blue whale drinks about 380 liters of milk every day!
Blue whales can be heard from over 1,000 miles away.
They can grow up to 100 feet long - that's as long as three school buses!

title: Octopus
facts: An octopus has three hearts and blue blood!
Octopuses can change their color in less than one second to hide from enemies.
They have eight arms, and each arm has its own mini brain!
An octopus can squeeze through any hole bigger than its beak - even a tiny one!
They squirt ink like a smoke screen to escape from danger.

title: Cheetah
facts: Cheetahs are the fastest animals on land - they can run as fast as a car on the highway!
A cheetah can go from standing still to super fast in just 3 seconds.
They have special claws that grip the ground like running shoes.
Cheetahs can't roar like lions - instead they chirp and purr like pet cats!
The black lines under their eyes help block the sun, like a football player's face paint.

title: Elephant
facts: Elephants are the largest animals that live on land - they can weigh as much as a school bus!
An elephant's trunk has over 40,000 muscles and can pick up something as tiny as a peanut.
Baby elephants suck their trunks just like human babies suck their thumbs!
Elephants can hear each other from miles away through vibrations in the ground felt by their feet.
They never forget! Elephants have amazing memories and can remember friends for decades.

title: Penguin
facts: Penguins can't fly in the air, but they 'fly' underwater at super speeds!
Emperor penguin dads keep the egg warm on their feet for two whole months without eating.
Penguins propose to each other with a special pebble - it's like giving a ring!
They can drink salt water because they have a special gland that filters out the salt.
Some penguins can hold their breath for over 20 minutes while diving.

title: Butterfly
facts: Butterflies taste with their feet! They land on food and know if it's yummy right away.
A butterfly starts life as a caterpillar and completely transforms inside a chrysalis.
Some butterflies fly over 3,000 miles to find warmer weather - that's like crossing the whole United States!
Their wings are covered in thousands of tiny scales that create beautiful colors.
Butterflies can see colors that humans can't even imagine, like ultraviolet light!

title: Dolphin
facts: Dolphins sleep with one eye open! Half their brain stays awake to watch for danger.
Each dolphin has its own special whistle - like having a name they call each other!
Dolphins can swim up to 20 miles per hour - faster than most people can ride a bike.
They use clicking sounds to 'see' underwater, like a superpower called echolocation.
Baby dolphins are born tail-first so they don't drown, and mom pushes them up for their first breath!

title: Giraffe
facts: Giraffes are the tallest animals on Earth - they're as tall as a two-story building!
Every giraffe has a unique pattern of spots, just like your fingerprints are unique to you.
A giraffe's tongue is about 20 inches long and is purple to protect it from sunburn!
Baby giraffes can stand up and walk within an hour of being born.
Giraffes only need about 30 minutes of sleep per day - imagine staying awake that long!

title: Owl
facts: Owls can turn their heads almost all the way around - about 270 degrees!
Their feathers are so soft that they can fly without making any sound at all.
Owls can't move their eyeballs - that's why they turn their whole head to look around.
A group of owls is called a parliament!
Some owls have ears that are at different heights on their head to help them pinpoint sounds perfectly.

title: Honeybee
facts: Honeybees do a special wiggle dance to tell their friends where to find flowers!
A single bee makes only about one-twelfth of a teaspoon of honey in its whole life.
Bees visit about 2 million flowers to make just one jar of honey!
Honeybees have five eyes - two big ones and three tiny ones on top of their head.
Honey never goes bad! Scientists found 3,000-year-old honey in Egypt and it was still good to eat.

title: Chameleon
facts: Chameleons can move each eye in a different direction at the same time!
They change color to show their mood - bright colors mean happy, dark colors mean grumpy.
A chameleon's tongue is often longer than its entire body and shoots out super fast!
Their tongue zooms out so fast it goes from 0 to 60 in one hundredth of a second.
Baby chameleons can change color from the moment they are born.

title: Kangaroo
facts: A baby kangaroo is called a joey and is only the size of a jellybean when it's born!
Kangaroos can jump 30 feet in one leap - that's longer than a school bus!
They can't walk backwards because of the shape of their big tail and legs.
Kangaroos use their strong tails like a third leg to balance when standing.
A group of kangaroos is called a mob!

title: Sea Turtle
facts: Sea turtles have been around since the time of the dinosaurs - over 100 million years!
Mother sea turtles return to the exact same beach where they were born to lay their eggs.
Some sea turtles can hold their breath for up to 5 hours while sleeping underwater!
Baby sea turtles follow the moonlight to find their way to the ocean after hatching.
The leatherback sea turtle can dive deeper than 3,000 feet - deeper than most submarines go!

title: Red Panda
facts: Red pandas are not actually related to giant pandas - they're more like raccoons!
They wrap their fluffy tails around themselves like a blanket to stay warm.
Red pandas spend most of their life up in trees and even sleep in branches.
They have a fake thumb on their paws that helps them grip bamboo stems.
Red pandas are so shy and quiet that scientists sometimes call them the 'Firefox'!

title: Hummingbird
facts: Hummingbirds can fly backwards - they're the only birds in the world that can do this!
Their wings beat up to 80 times per second - so fast you can only see a blur.
The bee hummingbird is the smallest bird in the world, lighter than a penny!
A hummingbird's heart beats over 1,200 times per minute - yours beats about 100.
They drink nectar from flowers using a tongue that flicks in and out 13 times per second.

title: Eiffel Tower
facts: The Eiffel Tower in Paris grows about 6 inches taller in summer because the metal expands in heat!
It was built in just 2 years for a World's Fair and was supposed to be torn down after 20 years.
There are 1,665 steps to climb to the very top!
The tower is repainted every 7 years and it takes 60 tons of paint - that's the weight of 10 elephants!
About 7 million people visit the Eiffel Tower every year - that's like the whole population of a small country.

title: Great Wall of China
facts: The Great Wall of China is over 13,000 miles long - you could wrap it around the Earth's middle!
It took over 2,000 years to build, with millions of workers helping over many centuries.
Some parts of the wall are wide enough for 5 horses to ride side by side!
The wall was built to protect China from invaders and has watchtowers all along it.
Sticky rice was mixed into the cement to help hold the bricks together!

title: Amazon Rainforest
facts: The Amazon Rainforest is so big it would cover most of the United States!
It makes about 20% of the world's oxygen - that's why it's called the 'Lungs of the Earth'.
One in ten of all the animal species in the world lives in the Amazon.
The trees grow so thick that when it rains, it takes 10 minutes for water to reach the ground!
New insects and animals are still being discovered there every single day.

title: Mount Everest
facts: Mount Everest is the tallest mountain on Earth - so high that airplanes fly at the same height!
It's still growing about half an inch taller every year because the ground underneath keeps pushing up.
The top of Everest is so high that there's only one-third as much air to breathe up there.
It takes most climbers about two months to reach the top because they have to get used to thin air.
There's so much snow and ice on top that it never melts, even in summer!

title: Grand Canyon
facts: The Grand Canyon is over a mile deep - you could stack 4 Eiffel Towers on top of each other inside it!
The Colorado River took about 5 to 6 million years to carve the canyon out of rock.
Some of the rocks at the bottom are almost 2 billion years old - half the age of the Earth!
It's so big you can see it from outer space.
The temperature at the top can be freezing cold while the bottom is super hot at the same time!

title: Great Barrier Reef
facts: The Great Barrier Reef is the largest living thing on Earth - you can even see it from space!
It's home to over 1,500 species of fish and 400 types of coral.
The reef is made by tiny animals called coral polyps that are smaller than your fingernail.
It stretches over 1,400 miles along Australia's coast - longer than the distance from New York to Miami!
Sea turtles, dolphins, and even whales visit the reef like a giant underwater neighborhood.

title: Sahara Desert
facts: The Sahara Desert is almost as big as the entire United States!
Even though it's super hot during the day, it can get freezing cold at night.
Only about 25% of the Sahara is sandy - the rest is rocky and gravelly ground.
Millions of years ago, the Sahara was actually a green forest with rivers and lakes!
Sand dunes in the Sahara can be as tall as a 60-story skyscraper.

title: Niagara Falls
facts: Over 3,000 tons of water flow over Niagara Falls every single second!
The falls are shared by two countries - the United States and Canada.
Niagara Falls is slowly moving backwards as the water wears away the rock - about one foot per year.
The mist from the falls can be seen from miles away and creates beautiful rainbows on sunny days.
People have gone over the falls in barrels - but it's very dangerous and not allowed anymore!

title: Antarctica
facts: Antarctica is the coldest place on Earth - temperatures can drop to minus 128 degrees!
About 70% of all the fresh water on Earth is frozen in Antarctica's ice sheets.
No country owns Antarctica - it belongs to the whole world for science and peace.
Even though it's covered in ice, Antarctica is actually a desert because it barely gets any rain or snow.
Penguins, seals, and whales live there, but no polar bears - polar bears only live at the North Pole!

title: Machu Picchu
facts: Machu Picchu is an ancient city built on top of a mountain in Peru, high up in the clouds!
It was built about 600 years ago by the Inca people without using any wheels or iron tools.
The stones fit together so perfectly that you can't fit a piece of paper between them!
It was hidden in the jungle for hundreds of years until an explorer found it in 1911.
Llamas still walk around Machu Picchu and help keep the grass trimmed like natural lawnmowers.

title: Taj Mahal
facts: The Taj Mahal was built by a king as a gift of love for his queen who passed away.
It took over 20,000 workers and 22 years to build!
The white marble changes color throughout the day - pinkish in the morning, white at noon, and golden at sunset.
Over 1,000 elephants helped carry the heavy marble stones used to build it.
Precious gems like diamonds, rubies, and sapphires are set into the walls in beautiful patterns.

title: Colosseum
facts: The Colosseum in Rome could hold 50,000 people - like a huge sports stadium today!
It was built almost 2,000 years ago by the Ancient Romans.
It had a giant canvas roof that could be pulled over the top to give shade on hot days.
The floor could be flooded with water to have pretend sea battles!
It took only 8 years to build, which was super fast for such a gigantic building.

title: Pyramids of Egypt
facts: The Great Pyramid was the tallest building in the world for over 3,800 years!
It's made of about 2.3 million stone blocks, each weighing as much as a car.
The pyramids are over 4,500 years old - they were ancient even to the Ancient Romans!
The four sides of the Great Pyramid line up almost perfectly with north, south, east, and west.
The inside temperature of the pyramid stays at exactly 68 degrees all the time, like natural air conditioning.

title: Yellowstone
facts: Yellowstone sits on top of a supervolcano with enough hot melted rock to fill the Grand Canyon 11 times!
Old Faithful geyser shoots boiling water up to 180 feet in the air about every 90 minutes.
The park has more geysers than any other place on Earth - over 500!
Rainbow-colored hot springs get their amazing colors from tiny living things called bacteria.
Yellowstone was the very first national park in the whole world, created in 1872.

title: Northern Lights
facts: The Northern Lights are caused by tiny particles from the Sun crashing into Earth's atmosphere!
They can be green, pink, purple, red, yellow, and blue - like a light show in the sky.
The lights happen about 60 miles above the ground - way higher than any airplane flies.
People in ancient times thought the lights were spirits dancing in the sky.
The Southern Hemisphere has them too - they're called the Southern Lights or Aurora Australis!

title: USA
facts: The USA flag has 50 stars - one for each state in the country!
The 13 red and white stripes represent the 13 original colonies that started America.
The flag has been changed 27 times as new states were added over the years.
When astronauts landed on the Moon, they planted an American flag there!
The current flag was designed by a 17-year-old student named Robert Heft for a school project.

title: Japan
facts: Japan's flag has a big red circle on a white background that represents the Sun.
Japan is called the 'Land of the Rising Sun' because the Sun appears to rise from there first.
The flag is one of the simplest and most recognizable flags in the whole world.
The red circle is called 'Hinomaru' which means 'circle of the Sun' in Japanese.
The white background stands for honesty and purity, and the red stands for warmth and brightness.

title: India
facts: India's flag has a spinning wheel called the Ashoka Chakra in the center with 24 spokes.
The saffron (orange) on top stands for courage, the white for peace, and the green for growth.
The 24 spokes on the wheel represent 24 hours in a day - the country is always moving forward!
The Indian flag must be made only from a special hand-spun cloth called khadi.
The wheel in the middle was inspired by an ancient emperor named Ashoka who loved peace.

title: Brazil
facts: Brazil's flag has a starry sky on it with 27 stars - one for each state!
The green background represents Brazil's amazing forests and nature.
The yellow diamond shape stands for Brazil's gold and wealth.
The stars on the flag show the exact sky that was visible over Rio de Janeiro on November 15, 1889.
The banner across the middle says 'Order and Progress' in Portuguese.

title: Australia
facts: Australia's flag has the Southern Cross constellation - stars you can only see from the Southern Hemisphere!
There's a big seven-pointed star called the Commonwealth Star under the British flag corner.
The six points on the Commonwealth Star represent the six states, plus one point for the territories.
The blue background represents the ocean that surrounds Australia on all sides.
Australia held a competition to design their flag and got over 30,000 entries!

title: Canada
facts: Canada's flag has a red maple leaf in the center - maple trees are everywhere in Canada!
The flag is nicknamed the 'Maple Leaf' and was first flown on February 15, 1965.
The maple leaf has exactly 11 points on it.
Canadians make most of the world's maple syrup - over 70%!
The two red stripes on the sides represent the Atlantic and Pacific oceans on Canada's east and west.

title: France
facts: France's flag has three vertical stripes: blue, white, and red - called the Tricolor!
The three colors stand for liberty, equality, and brotherhood.
The French flag inspired many other countries to use three-stripe designs for their flags.
During the French Revolution, people wore ribbons with these three colors to show they wanted freedom.
The Tricolor has been France's flag since 1794 - that's over 200 years!

title: South Africa
facts: South Africa's flag has six colors - more than almost any other flag in the world!
The Y-shape in the flag represents different groups of people coming together as one nation.
The flag was created in 1994 when South Africa became a free country for all its people.
It was designed in just one week before it needed to be used!
South Africa is called the 'Rainbow Nation' because it has so many different cultures and people.

title: Mexico
facts: Mexico's flag has an eagle sitting on a cactus eating a snake right in the middle!
The eagle on the cactus comes from an ancient Aztec legend about where to build their city.
Green stands for hope, white for unity, and red for the blood of heroes.
Mexico's flag looks similar to Italy's but has the eagle symbol that makes it unique.
September 24th is Flag Day in Mexico, and people have big celebrations!

title: Nepal
facts: Nepal is the only country in the world whose flag is NOT a rectangle - it's two stacked triangles!
The flag looks like two little pennants on top of each other.
The crimson red color represents bravery, and the blue border means peace.
One triangle has a moon and the other has a sun, meaning Nepal will last as long as they do!
The unusual shape represents the Himalaya Mountains and the two main religions in Nepal.

title: United Kingdom
facts: The UK flag is called the Union Jack and combines the flags of three countries into one!
It mixes the crosses of England (red cross), Scotland (white X on blue), and Ireland (red X).
The flag is NOT symmetrical - if you look closely, the red diagonal stripes are slightly off-center!
The name 'Jack' might come from the small flag flown on the front of ships.
The Union Jack has been used since 1801 - that's over 200 years!

title: Italy
facts: Italy's flag has three vertical stripes: green, white, and red - like the colors of pizza toppings!
The green represents the beautiful hills, white represents the snowy mountains, and red represents volcanoes.
Italy's flag was inspired by the French flag when Napoleon visited Italy.
The flag is sometimes called 'Il Tricolore' which means 'The Three Colors' in Italian.
Italy is shaped like a boot, and the flag flies proudly from the top to the toe!

title: China
facts: China's flag has one big star and four smaller stars on a bright red background.
The big star represents the government, and the four small stars represent the people of China.
Red is a very lucky and important color in Chinese culture!
The yellow color of the stars represents the golden future and also the Yellow River.
China's flag was first raised on October 1, 1949, in Tiananmen Square in Beijing.

title: Kenya
facts: Kenya's flag has a cool Maasai warrior shield and two spears right in the center!
The black stripe represents the people, red for the struggle for freedom, and green for the land.
The white stripes stand for peace and honesty.
The shield and spears mean Kenyans will defend their freedom.
Kenya is famous for its amazing wildlife - lions, elephants, and zebras roam there!

title: Argentina
facts: Argentina's flag has a golden Sun of May in the center with a face on it!
The sun represents the Inca sun god and a new nation being born.
The light blue and white stripes are said to represent the sky and clouds.
The sun has 32 rays - 16 straight ones and 16 wavy ones that go back and forth!
Legend says the flag's creator saw the sun break through the clouds on an important day.

title: Sun
facts: The Sun is so big that about 1.3 million Earths could fit inside it!
Light from the Sun takes about 8 minutes to reach Earth - so when you see sunshine, it's already 8 minutes old.
The Sun is a giant ball of super hot gas - the surface is about 10,000 degrees!
The Sun is about 4.6 billion years old, which is about halfway through its life.
Without the Sun, Earth would be a frozen dark ball floating in space.

title: Moon
facts: The Moon is slowly drifting away from Earth - about 1.5 inches farther every year!
Footprints left by astronauts on the Moon will stay there for millions of years because there's no wind.
The Moon doesn't make its own light - it reflects light from the Sun like a giant mirror.
Only 12 people have ever walked on the Moon, and they were all Americans.
The Moon has no sound at all because there's no air to carry sound waves.

title: Mars
facts: Mars is called the Red Planet because it's covered in rusty iron dust!
A day on Mars is almost the same as Earth - just 37 minutes longer.
Mars has the biggest volcano in the solar system - Olympus Mons is three times taller than Mount Everest!
Mars has two tiny moons named Phobos and Deimos, which mean 'Fear' and 'Panic' in Greek.
Scientists have found signs that water used to flow on Mars millions of years ago.

title: Jupiter
facts: Jupiter is the biggest planet in our solar system - over 1,300 Earths could fit inside!
Jupiter's Great Red Spot is a giant storm that has been raging for over 350 years!
That storm is so big that two or three Earths could fit inside it.
Jupiter has at least 95 moons - it's like a mini solar system!
A year on Jupiter lasts about 12 Earth years, but a day is only 10 hours long.

title: Saturn's Rings
facts: Saturn's rings are made of billions of pieces of ice and rock, some as tiny as grains of sand and some as big as houses!
The rings stretch out really far but are super thin - only about 30 feet thick in some places!
Saturn could float in a giant bathtub because it's less dense than water!
The rings might disappear in about 100 million years as they slowly fall into the planet.
Saturn has over 140 moons - one of them, Titan, is bigger than the planet Mercury!

title: Stars
facts: There are more stars in the universe than grains of sand on all of Earth's beaches!
Stars twinkle because their light wiggles as it passes through Earth's air.
Our Sun is actually a star - it just looks bigger because it's the closest one to us!
Some stars are so far away that their light takes millions of years to reach your eyes.
When you look at the night sky, some of the stars you see might not exist anymore!

title: Milky Way
facts: Our galaxy, the Milky Way, has between 100 and 400 billion stars in it!
It got its name because it looks like a splash of spilled milk across the night sky.
If you could travel at the speed of light, it would take 100,000 years to cross the Milky Way!
Our solar system is spinning around the center of the Milky Way at 515,000 miles per hour.
The Milky Way is shaped like a giant pinwheel spinning through space.

title: Astronauts
facts: Astronauts grow about 2 inches taller in space because there's no gravity squishing their spine!
They have to exercise 2 hours every day in space or their muscles and bones would get weak.
In space, astronauts see 16 sunrises and 16 sunsets every single day!
Astronaut food comes in special pouches - they can even eat ice cream that's freeze-dried!
Tears don't fall in space - they just stick to your face in a blob because there's no gravity!

title: International Space Station
facts: The International Space Station is as big as a football field and you can see it from Earth with your eyes!
It zooms around Earth at 17,500 miles per hour - fast enough to circle the whole planet in 90 minutes!
People from 19 different countries helped build the space station together.
Astronauts have been living on the ISS nonstop since the year 2000!
It's the most expensive thing ever built, costing over 100 billion dollars.

title: Mercury
facts: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun but is NOT the hottest - Venus is!
A year on Mercury is only 88 Earth days, but one day on Mercury lasts 59 Earth days!
Mercury is the smallest planet in our solar system - only a little bigger than our Moon.
It has no atmosphere, so it goes from super hot (800°F) to super cold (-290°F)!
Mercury is covered in craters from space rocks crashing into it, just like our Moon.

title: Venus
facts: Venus is the hottest planet in our solar system - hot enough to melt lead!
It spins backwards compared to most other planets, so the Sun rises in the west there.
Venus is sometimes called Earth's twin because they're almost the same size.
A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus - that's mind-blowing!
Venus is the brightest planet in our sky - you can see it shining at sunrise or sunset.

title: Neptune
facts: Neptune is the windiest planet - winds blow at over 1,200 miles per hour!
It takes Neptune 165 Earth years to go around the Sun once - that's a really long year!
Neptune is so far away that it took the Voyager 2 spacecraft 12 years to reach it.
It's a beautiful deep blue color because of a gas called methane in its atmosphere.
Neptune has 16 known moons, and the biggest one, Triton, orbits backwards!

title: Comets
facts: Comets are like giant dirty snowballs made of ice, dust, and rock flying through space!
When a comet gets close to the Sun, the ice melts and creates a glowing tail millions of miles long.
Some comets take thousands of years to orbit the Sun just once!
The tail of a comet always points away from the Sun, pushed by solar wind.
Halley's Comet visits Earth about every 75 years - it will come back in 2061!

title: Black Holes
facts: Black holes have gravity so strong that nothing can escape - not even light!
If you fell into a black hole, you would stretch out like spaghetti - scientists actually call it 'spaghettification'!
The black hole at the center of our Milky Way is 4 million times heavier than our Sun!
Time moves slower near a black hole - if you visited one and came back, everyone else would be much older!
Black holes are invisible, but scientists can see them by watching how they bend light around them.

title: Earth
facts: Earth is the only planet we know of that has life - from tiny bacteria to giant whales!
About 71% of Earth's surface is covered in water, which is why it looks blue from space.
Earth is spinning at over 1,000 miles per hour right now, but you can't feel it!
Our planet is about 4.5 billion years old - if Earth's history were a day, humans would appear in the last second before midnight.
Earth has a giant magnet inside it that protects us from harmful rays from the Sun, like an invisible shield!
